name: Mechanical Shrimp Charm
summary: Its a shrimp

name: Benchy Board
description:
I made this Benchy Board for a YouTube short. It does actually slide down snow pretty well! Might need some wax...

Based off a real snowboard profile, made in Fusion!

Printing instructions:

Layer height: .2

Infill: 15%

Supports: Only for front and rear of board. 3MF has painting data.

You are likely to encounter some bad artifacts around the area where the board starts to bend upward on the front and back. This is where the slicer has to decide what to bridge and what to support. Watch these layers closely, and please comment if you have any solutions!
summary: Send your Benchy down the slopes on its very own Snowboard!

name: Toyota Heritage Tri-Color Badge - Grille, Keychain, Decal - Parametric
description:
This model was inspired by classic Toyota Tri-Color racing grille accessories. It is designed to be printed as an adhesive decal, a keychain, or a grille accessory. 

There are models for both single and multi-color printers in respective folders below. 

 

For single color printers:

Print your body of choice (regular, keychain, grille) and then the corresponding "nodes" in your desired color scheme. For the grille accessory, there are little divots in the nodes that are there to give the zip ties room to fit. There should be enough tolerance for these nodes to fit. Use super glue to adhere these onto the body of the accessory.

 

For multi color printers:

A sample .3mf contains the 3 different styles of accessory. Change colors and materials to your liking.

 

This model is parametric, specifically for the distance between the zip tie slots on the grille accessories.

All files have a .step and .f3d counterpart. For all grille accessories Fusion files, there exists a parameter called ZipTieSpacing, which you can change depending on what style of grille you have. For a 2016 Toyota Tacoma, this is 1 inch. 

You are free to modify this design however you like to fit your grille! I'd start with the Tri Color Accessory .f3d file and design a mounting system on the back.

 

Print settings:

  • Layer height: .2mm
  • Supports: none
  • Infill: 100% OR 8+ Top solid layers (for color consistency)
  • Ironing: On 

Again, the grille accessory is designed to be printed without supports. The printer should be able to bridge the internal geometry. 

For attaching the grille accessory to your grille, insert two 2.5x150 generic nylon zip ties, then tie around desired points as seen in the pictures below:
summary: Tri Color Accessory ready to be mounted to your grille, printed as a keychain, or adhered as a decal

name: HP Smart Tank Bottle Tray
description:
Use this tabletop tray to store your HP Smart Tank Ink Bottles. Labeled CMYK for each bottle type. 

Includes 10mm holes on bottom for 10mm rubber adhesive grips.
summary: Designed for 31 HP Smart Tank Ink Bottles

name: Fix-It Felix Hammer
description:
Fix-It Felix hammer in two sizes with CAD files made in Fusion! Jumbo is comically large scale and regular is designed to more closely resemble a regular hammer size. 

Printed in Silk Gold PLA with PETG supports, upside down.

Enjoy!
summary: Fix-It Felix Hammer with Text, CAD Files, .3mf
category: Costumes & Accessories > Cosplay & Costumes in general

name: Hollow Knight Silksong Hornet Umbrella
description:
As seen in: 


This model is a replica of Hornet's head from Hollow Knight Silksong, and is designed to specifically work with

The Spectrum Popular Style 42" Automatic Open Compact Travel Umbrella

The model features an interface on the bottom which is designed to be superglued onto the “cap” of the umbrella. When printed in PETG and glued with generic super glue, this model stuck very well!

There is also a blank version for you to add your own interface to your own umbrella, if desired.

Recommended print settings are stored in the .3mf below
summary: Umbrella topper to make your ordinary red umbrella into Hornet from Hollow Knight!

She got shoes on!!!


Used a bit of fabric to make her lil shirt

name: Parametric Grip Strength Trainer Stand
description:
I designed this simple stand for you to store your grip trainers in an aesthetic and functional way. This was designed specifically for the Captains of Crush grip trainer set, with options to print it as being able to fit one, three, five, and ten trainers.

This model was uploaded with an accompanying .f3d file in which there is a parameter you can change to set this to your desired storage amount. It can be whatever you like!
summary: Designed for the Captains of Crush grip strength trainers

name: Sports Acessory Storage Rack V2 - Inspired by TONS Gear Organizers
description:
Overview:

This system was designed due to popular demand as a second version of my Bike Acessory Storage Rack. This style of storage rack is inspired by the Tons Gear Organizer product. It is designed to work with a 1" (specifically, 25.4 mm) wooden dowel to fasten onto your wall in either a horizontal or vertical format, or sit on your tabletop. With the addition of more hooks and accessories, this system has been expanded to include more sports like running, climbing, and more.

This is a live service and open source model. Your feedback will be accounted for and updates will be made! I encourage you to customize it as you'd like! The Generic Bar Attachment is a great starting point to make your accessory of choice.

All files are downloadable in .STL, .STEP, and .F3D format so you can customize them to your liking in your software of choice. 

Almost all of these accessories are compatible with the Tons Gear Organizer system. Apply a 110% scale in Prusa Slicer to each part to make it fit on a standard Tons bar. 

Each part should also be compatible with V1, apply a 96% scale in Prusa Slicer to make it compatible.

Materials:

  • Recommended print material: ASA, ABS, PETG-CF
  • 1" Wooden Dowel
  • Generic drywall screws
  • Generic drywall anchors
  • 1" Rubber O Rings (for keeping vertical accessories in place)
  • M4 Screws
  • M4 Nuts

Print instructions for computer holders, wall fasteners, table stands, and helmet mounts:

  • Support may be necessary on horizontal components
  • .1-.2 mm layer height
  • High infill / 2+ wall perimeters (you want it to be strong!)

Print instructions for all hooks:

  • .2-.3 mm layer height
  • All walls. No infill. The entire print should be walls. This makes the print faster and stronger! (you can accomplish this by setting perimeters to a high number like 10)

.3mf example can also be found in the Print Files folder.

 

Assembly instructions can be found in the description of each item under their download. For the wall attachments with screws, please reference the below diagrams for how to assemble using M4 fasteners
summary: Version 2 of my Bike Acessory Storage Rack inspired by Tons Gear Organizers, with new accessories!

name: The bloc ecosystem - CNC enclosure, controller, guide
description:
All info about this project can be found at blocnc.com

As makers and engineers, we know how thrilling it is to turn ideas into reality—but we also know how intimidating fabrication tools can be for beginners. That’s why we designed bloc, a user-friendly CNC system that strips away the complexity and makes digital fabrication accessible to everyone.

With a gamified interface and joystick controls, bloc makes creating fun and intuitive, no CAD experience required. Safety is built in, with features like an enclosed system, automatic cleanup, and guided workflows. It’s versatile too – supporting materials like cardboard, wood, and acrylic—so whether you're prototyping at home or teaching in a classroom, bloc adapts to your needs.

And as your skills grow, bloc grows with you, providing a pathway into CAD and digital design without overwhelming you from the start. We’re not just building machines—we’re empowering the next generation of makers to bring their ideas to life.

What you'll need for this project:

Fasteners

https://a.co/d/bxtpEl4

Heat Inserts

https://a.co/d/9EYS3Rx

CNC Machine

https://a.co/d/hov89tV

Arduino / electronics

https://a.co/d/bz6Qkhk

Emergency stop

https://a.co/d/8DrPIo0

Limit switches

https://a.co/d/3atWTiT

Ball end mill

https://a.co/d/jhT4AQ5

Generic hinges

n/a

More (WIP)
summary: Desktop CNC ecosystem enclosure, controller, and innovation guide designed for generic 3018 Pro CNC machine

name: Lunar Standstill Model
description:
This model, designed by Dr. Erica Ellingson and modeled by Andrew Widner (me) at the University of Colorado is designed to illustrate a lunar standstill. It is designed for astronomy education, with teaching notes below, written by Dr. Erica. Printing and assembly instructions are attached.

 

Lunar Standstill Model Teaching Notes:

 

Hold the model so that the North/South axis is up/down (the handle will be slightly tilted). identify North and South directions and how objects can be seen in the sky farther or less far north.

 

This can be seen most easily by watching the rising or setting positions. Imagine a horizon (or use the real one) and point your hand in different directions to indicate “more north” and “more south” rise/set positions. This is an important set-up for what comes next.

 

Optional: The flat plane around the Earth is the plane where the Earth orbits the Sun each year. You can think of this as the apparent path of the Sun around our sky across the year, aka the ecliptic.

 

The Moon’s orbit is tilted from the plane of the Earth’s orbit by about 5 degrees- this is exaggerated in the model! Note also that the moon is MUCH closer to the Earth in the model than in reality. Hold the Moon’s orbit steady and move the moon around (counterclockwise as seen from the top) and note how it goes north and south each month (moonth).

 

Optional: you can demonstrate the phases of the Moon as seen from the Earth by identifying where the sunlight is coming from (use the real sun if you can!).

 

The Moon’s orbit “regresses” backwards around the Earth. Turn the moon’s orbit (clockwise as seen from the top). This cycle takes 18.6 years.

 

Ask your students: find the configuration when the Moon will be as far north as it can possibly go- this is the “northern lunar extreme” position that only happens during the “major lunar standstill season”. They should be able to figure out how to rotate the Moon’s orbit so that the orbital tilt aligns with the Earth’s N/S axis.

 

Run your finger around the Moon’s orbit to show how the northernmost extreme is followed by the southernmost extreme ½ month later. Sweep your hand horizontally “north and south” to indicate a wide and fast swing of the position of moonrise or moonset along the horizon from day to day.

 

Rotate the Moon’s orbit to the “minor lunar standstill” position, 9 years later. Re-identify North and South and show how the Moon now never goes very far north or south, in comparison. Sweep your hand along the horizon in a smaller range to indicate the swing of the moon on the minor standstill. Finish with demonstrating the pattern of the Moon’s monthly swing getting wider and then narrower and then wider over 18.6 years.

 

Optional: the “nodes” mark where the Moon and Sun orbits intersect and the technical term for this cycle is the “Regression of the nodes.” Eclipses happen when the Sun and Moon are both on the nodes. You can use the model to show that there are two eclipse seasons each year, each one having one lunar and one solar eclipse (and sometimes a third). The regression of the nodes means that the seasons are separated by slightly less than ½ year.
summary: Model demonstrating the path of the moon around the earth

name: Bike Accessory Storage Rack - Inspired by Tons Gear Organizers
description:
V2 of this model is now up! Go check it out

Overview:

This system was designed for a college independent study and was inspired by the Tons Gear Organizer product. It is designed to work with a roughly 1" (specifically, 24.6 mm) wooden dowel to fasten onto your wall in either a horizontal or vertical format. 

If this model doesn't fit on your 1" bar (if its actually truly 1" and not 24.6) then apply a 103% scale in Prusa Slicer, and it should fit.

Materials:

  • Recommended print material: ASA, ABS, PETG
  • ~1" Wooden Dowel
  • Generic drywall screws
  • Generic drywall anchors
  • M3 Screws (optional)
  • M3 Nuts (optional)

Print instructions:

  • No support
  • .1-.2 mm layer height
  • Brim (hooks, glasses holder)
  • High infill / 2+ wall perimeters (you want it to be strong!)

 

Assembly instructions

 

Glasses holder:

The glasses holder is designed to be used with a vertically mounted dowel. It is recommended to install the holder before installing the dowel on the wall. Slide the holder onto the dowel before installing the dowel into the wall. Then, to achieve your desired level of tightness, insert an M3 nut into the hexagonal hole on the rear interface of the holder, then screw an M3 screw through the hole and nut to secure the holder in place.

Computer holder:

The computer holder is designed to be used with a vertically mounted dowel. It is recommended to install the holder before installing the dowel on the wall. Slide the holder onto the dowel before installing the dowel into the wall. Then, to achieve your desired level of tightness, insert an M3 nut into the hexagonal hole on the rear interface of the holder, then screw an M3 screw through the hole and nut to secure the holder in place

Helmet hook:

The helmet hook is designed to be used with a horizontally mounted dowel. It can be installed before or after the dowel has already been mounted. To attach the hook onto the dowel, find the arrow on the back of the hook, ensure that it is pointing up ^, then snap the hook onto the dowel. 

Shoe hook:

The shoe hook is designed to be used with a horizontally mounted dowel. It can be installed before or after the dowel has already been mounted. To attach the hook onto the dowel, find the arrow on the back of the hook, ensure that it is pointing up ^, then snap the hook onto the dowel. 

Wall fastener (2 part):

The 2 part wall fastener is designed to mount the dowel to the wall in either a horizontal or vertical format. First, use your desired length of drywall screw and anchor to mount two of the “Wall Fastener Part 1” on to the wall, ensuring they are level / aligned with each other. Next, insert M3 nuts into the hexagonal slots in each “Wall Fastener Part 2”. Then, slide each “Wall Fastener Part 2” onto the wooden dowel. Last, use an M3 screw to align and screw each of the two wall fastener parts to each other, and tighten to your desired tightness.

One part wall fastener:

The one part wall fastener is designed to mount the dowel to the wall in either a horizontal or vertical format. First, use your desired length of drywall screw and anchor to mount two of the “One part wall fastener” on to the wall, ensuring they are level / aligned with each other. Then, brace the wall fasteners as you slide the wooden dowel through each wall fastener.

Perpendicular wall mount:

The perpendicular wall mount can be screwed into drywall / studs, and is designed to have a very tight tolerance between the dowel and 3D printed interface. It is recommended to use a mallet to fully insert your desired length of dowel into the perpendicular wall mount.
summary: This bike accessory storage system uses a 1" diameter wooden dowel to store your biking gear in an aesthetic wall mount

name: Bear Holding Sign
description:
I sculpted this bear as a gift! It was made in Blender (.blend file attached) and is designed to be remixed into your own personalized home decorations or gifts.

Open one of my 3mf files, then use the coloring tool in Prusa Slicer to color in your desired text. Or, feel free to model some text into the board and upload a remix using Prusa Slicer's text emboss tool!
summary: Color or emboss your phrase of choice into the sign of this happy bear!

name: Hexagonal Basalt Column Rock Formation "Chair"
description:
I designed this model using Grasshopper, the file of which is linked below. Bare in mind, I am not very good at Grasshopper so please bear with the poor optimization. 

 

This model was designed to look like the naturally occurring basalt rock formations in Iceland, and were part of a larger college assignment to create ‘furniture’ using 3D printing. Unfortunately, I don't imagine my design is very comfortable… but it looks cool!

 

You can read my assignment summary here, as well as download the Grasshopper file, if you're curious!

https://www.notion.so/Mini-3-A-Seat-at-the-Table-12c584a03f2f80e0ab11d54a6c760efd
summary: Designed in Grasshopper to mimic Icelandic Basalt rock formations

name: Decorative Stand - No Supports
description:
I made this stand in Fusion360 for a friend who had gotten a decorative knife souvenir from Pompeii, Italy. 

While the part does not require supports, it is recommended to print it with a low layer height to ensure the layers are nice and clean on the sloped sections.

The diameter of the fork is 22.278 mm. Scale the stand to the size of your choice!
summary: Good for displaying your item of choice! Does not require supports

name: Toyota Truck Rail Clamp For Sub Zero Car Scraper
description: Designed to fit a Sub Zero Snow Scraper onto the rails on the back of a 3rd gen Toyota Tacoma. Recommended to print solid and out of a UV resistant material like ASA.
summary: This model is designed to fit a Sub Zero Scraper with a diameter of 35mm onto the Toyota Tacoma Truck Bed T-Clip

name: Toyota Tacoma Truck Bed Rail T-Clip
description:
This model was developed with the purpose of creating a modular system that can be used to mount things on the truck bed rails of a 3rd gen Toyota Tacoma.

Developed in association with LineArcLine, the system features a T-clip system that uses M4 screws and nuts to fasten tightly onto the rail.

This model is designed to work with standard M4 nuts and bolts, specifically a m4x25mm bolt. To use the model effectively, insert an M4 nut into each of the slots. Then, orient the T-clip so that the nuts will be pulling towards each other when tightened, as shown in this picture:

Then, insert the 25mm m4 bolt into the outside facing piece, screwing it almost all the way through. Screw the flat side of the inside piece into the bolt just until it gets halfway through the bolt.

Then, its a matter of fitting the t-nut in sideways, twisting it clockwise so it is vertical, and tightening the bolt. This can be achieved by either spinning the outside piece until it is tight or just tightening the bolt directly. This part can be tricky.

After that, you are done!

It is highly recommended to print this part out of a high temperature / UV resistant material. ASA is recommended. 

You are encouraged to remix this model to your hearts content!
summary: Designed in collaboration with @LineArcLine, this T-clip system attaches onto the rear rails of a 3rd gen Toyota Tacoma!

name: Skadis Parametric Desk Attachment
description:
This is a fitment I made for my oddly thick desk that I chose to have my 3D printers on. I made it parametric so others can use it as well!

Included in the downloads section of this file is a .f3d file. To make this work for your own desk, it's important that you change the dimensions of this model!

First, open the F360 file and navigate to the ‘Change Parameters’ tab. Then, under ‘User Parameters’, find the variables named the variables named DeskThickness and ClampDistance.

Change these parameters to the desired thickness of your desk, as well as the desired distance that the clamp will protrude onto the desk. Everyone's printer / desk is different, so find a tolerance value that works for you! 

I recommend printing this out of PETG, as it may be a little bit grippier than PLA or others. Enjoy, and make sure to give some love to Line Arc Line's original model!

To download the clip for this model, go to https://www.printables.com/model/256896-skadis-t-clip-system
summary: Parametric Desk clamp for Skadis peg board from Ikea. Remixed from Line Arc Line

name: Henry the Bathtime Whale
description:
Henry is just a little guy!

Henry is a simple little whale that is meant to sit on the side of the bath tub or swim around! Henry is watertight, but to make sure he stays safe, print him in high quality so that no mold can get in. Experiment with different infill types to make him float! Scale to whatever side you'd like!

Photos are renders from blender
summary: Just a little whale for bathtime! There are lots of little whales out there, but this is Henry!

description: This is the second version of my USB dongle protector, made specifically for two USB dongles on the back of my Lenovo LOQ laptop. One is made to cover the ethernet port, another, not. I'd recommend using the smaller one in most cases, as both the holes are 14mm, whereas the longer version is fit towards my computer with 14.5 mm for the dongle on the left.
